scan.access_pattern: sequential contiguous row partitions; each worker prefaults and scans its own fixed range so page placement and scan bandwidth stay local, with the shipdate predicate applied inline and no zonemap classification pass

data_structures.aggregation: per-thread uint8_t key_to_slot[65536] initialized to 0xFF plus compact AggState slots[8] and populated_keys[8]; merge only populated groups

optimization_notes: The root cause is a parallel scan bandwidth bottleneck after earlier fixes removed the oversized aggregation state: Q1 still streams about 2.28 GB of column data for 59.99M rows, and the remaining gap is dominated by page placement, thread scheduling, and memory-channel saturation rather than hash lookup or block pruning. Strategy category D: drop the non-selective zonemap path, keep the tiny cache-resident direct aggregation table, and run one static contiguous-partition scan with a fixed bandwidth-oriented thread count and parallel prefault/first-touch so each worker faults and scans the same slice, reducing remote-memory traffic and scheduler overhead while preserving the validated date arithmetic and revenue formula.
